methodology: Cross-reference network analysis (8-book cross-testamental chain) + patristic commentary reading (11 evidences: Jerome, Peter Chrysologus, Athanasius, Bernard of Clairvaux, Chrysostom, Augustine, Lapide, Methodius, Haydock, Bede) + CCEL works (Gill 122 sermons on Song, Calvin, Barnes) + topic verification (BRIDE, BRIDEGROOM, HUSBAND, MARRIAGE, BETROTHAL)

evidence: commentaries (Augustine, Lapide 1600+ chars, Chrysostom, Cyprian, Peter Chrysologus, Caesarius — 12 entries!)

relevance_change.reason: 12 patristic commentaries — richest verse in this query. Lapide writes 1600+ chars synthesizing Canticles, Rev 21:2, Jerome, Augustine. Augustine develops the virgin-mother paradox. Peter Chrysologus creates a direct cross-commentary chain: Hos 2:19 → Jn 3:29 → 2Co 11:2.

evidence: commentaries (Jerome '3 betrothals', Peter Chrysologus, Ambrose, Haydock — 7 entries)
ccel (Gill — 122 sermons on Song of Solomon)

new_refs_added.justification: 10 votes de REV.19.7. Climax escatológico da metáfora nupcial: 'a nova Jerusalém, ataviada como noiva adornada para o seu esposo.' Bede, Haydock, Johnson comentam. Verso que RESOLVE a metáfora — a noiva finalmente adornada e apresentada.

6 votes para MAT.9.15. 3 votes de 2CO.11.2. Agostinho, Lapide, Cirilo, Crisóstomo comentam. Verso onde Jesus é IDENTIFICADO como noivo por João Batista: 'O que tem a noiva é o noivo; o amigo do noivo se alegra.' Peter Chrysologus cita diretamente ao comentar Os 2:19.

5 votes de 2CO.11.2. Metódio (Adão/Eva como tipo Cristo/Igreja), Jerônimo, Agostinho, Dídimo comentam. 'Grande é este mistério, mas eu me refiro a Cristo e à igreja.' Verso que NOMEIA a metáfora como mysterion.

candidates_noted.justification: 4 votes de 2CO.11.2. 3 votes de EPH.5.25. Haydock: 'dois esposos — sinagoga e Igreja.' OT declaration: 'teu Criador é teu marido.'
6 votes para REV.19.7. Lapide: 'desposório nesta vida, casamento eterno na glória.' Parable of the ten virgins — readiness of the bride.
11 votes de REV.19.7. 'A esposa, a mulher do Cordeiro.' Final explicit identification.

cross_testamental_span.description: Most cross-testamental query in the entire set — 8 books connected
cross_testamental_span.chain: Hosea → Isaiah → Song of Solomon → Matthew → John → Ephesians → 2 Corinthians → Revelation
cross_testamental_span.significance_for_tcc: Tests whether hybrid search can connect OT marriage covenant theology (Hosea) through wisdom poetry (Song) to NT ecclesiology (Ephesians) and eschatology (Revelation). Pure lexical for 'noiva' finds only verses with that exact word; semantic must understand the marriage metaphor across vocabulary variants: 'desposar', 'esposa', 'mulher do Cordeiro', 'virgem pura'.

framework: 3. Cross/Resurrection — betrothal in faith and gospel grace
jerome_three_betrothals.significance: Maps the entire salvation history as progressive betrothal, culminating in the cross

methodius_adam_eve_typology.source: Methodius of Olympus, Commentary on Ephesians 5:31-32
methodius_adam_eve_typology.typology: Adam slept → Eve from his side | Christ died → Church from his wounded side
methodius_adam_eve_typology.quote: 'The Word left his Father in heaven. He came down to be bonded with this woman, the church. Then he fell into the sleep of his passion.'

augustine_virgin_mother_paradox.paradox: The Church is simultaneously virgin-bride AND fruitful mother — parallel with Mary
augustine_virgin_mother_paradox.quote: 'She is a virgin and she also brings forth children. She imitates Mary, who gave birth to the Lord.'
